title: Chicken Shawarma

overview.description: Juicy Middle Eastern spiced chicken thighs marinated with yoghurt, lemon and warm spices, then roasted until lightly charred.

method: Combine the yoghurt, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic and all the spices.
Coat the chicken thoroughly in the marinade.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours, preferably overnight.
Arrange the chicken on a wire rack over a roasting tray.
Roast for 30–35 minutes until cooked through and beginning to char around the edges.
Slice the chicken into thin strips.
Return the sliced chicken to the roasting tray and roast for a further 5 minutes to crisp the edges.

learning.did-you-know: Shawarma is believed to have originated in the Ottoman Empire and spread throughout the Levant, where each country developed its own spice blend and accompaniments.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a balanced shawarma marinade and recreate the crispy edges of rotisserie shawarma in a home oven.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A restaurant favourite that uses inexpensive chicken thighs, freezes well and pairs with several other recipes already in the Chapman Kitchen.
chapman-tips: Chicken thighs stay much juicier than breast in this recipe.
Marinating overnight makes a noticeable difference.
Roasting the sliced chicken for a final few minutes creates the characteristic crispy shawarma edges.
A wire rack allows hot air to circulate and gives more even browning.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze cooked sliced chicken in meal-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Excellent in wraps, salads, rice bowls or alongside hummus.
